summary: Excellent soundboard capture of an electrifying 1981 New Year's run featuring standout Deep Elem, Samson & Delilah and Jerry’s fiery guitar work.
review: The show is hailed as a high‑water mark of the late‑80s era – a solid first set, a second set that glows with Deep Elem Blues, Sugaree and an exquisite To Lay Me Down, and a third set many call “off the charts.”  The soundboard recording is praised for its clarity and balance, though listeners wish they could hear more of the audience ambience.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise (mostly 4‑5 stars) for both performance and audio quality across ten reviews; strong weighting toward an exceptional show with excellent soundboard recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced mix; reviewers note great clarity and “killer sound,” only missing the ambient audience presence.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Solid first set, a second set that “glows” with deep jams and standout tracks, and a third set described as off‑the‑charts – overall a well‑paced marathon show.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for fiery, finest‑ever guitar work (Deep Elem, Captain on fire, top‑notch solos).
